channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Future of Captive Orcas Wikie and Keijo Remains Uncertain After French Park Closure
item.description: Wikie and Keijo, the last two captive orcas in France, remain in their shuttered home at Marineland Antibes over a year after it closed due to financial difficulties and new legislation banning the ca...

item.title: Summer McIntosh Dominates Florida Meet with Strong Freestyle Performances
item.description: Canadian swimmer Summer McIntosh, 19, continues her dominance in the pool, showcasing her exceptional form at the Fort Lauderdale Open. In the 400-metre freestyle, McIntosh battled American swimming l...

item.title: KNBS report shows major funding shortfall and governance issues in Kenya’s Social Health Insurance Fund
item.description: A new Economic Survey 2026 by the Kenya National Bureau of Statistics (KNBS) has revealed serious financial strain within Kenya’s Social Health Insurance Fund (SHIF), raising concerns about its sustai...

item.title: Mali’s Defence Minister Dies in Coordinated Jihadist and Separatist Attacks
item.description: Mali don waka enter serious wahala as their Defence Minister, Sadio Camara, don die after coordinated attacks wey jihadist and separatist groups launch across the country. The attack come heavy for th...

item.title: Tamil Nadu Assembly Election Counting to Begin with Enhanced Security Measures
item.description: The Election Commission of India (ECI) has made extensive arrangements for the counting of votes in the Tamil Nadu Assembly Elections 2026, scheduled for May 4. A three-tier security system has been i...

item.title: President and Prime Minister Reaffirm Commitment to Press Freedom on World Press Freedom Day
item.description: On World Press Freedom Day, President Asif Ali Zardari and Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif expressed the government's commitment to upholding press freedom and emphasized the importance of credible jour...

item.title: How to Start Property Investment in South Africa with Minimal Capital
item.description: Property investment in South Africa no longer requires millions in capital or a hefty bank loan. Real Estate Investment Trusts (REITs) offer an accessible route for ordinary investors to own parts of ...

item.title: Rift Valley Leads in Voter Registration but President Ruto Warns of Low Youth Participation
item.description: The Rift Valley region has emerged as the top area in Kenya for new voter registration, recording 671,958 new voters in the recent enhanced registration exercise. Uasin Gishu led the counties with 64,...

item.title: Understanding the Growing Market and Trade of Ube
item.description: Ube, a purple yam native to the Philippines, has recently surged in popularity across the internet, appearing in everything from desserts to trendy café offerings. Behind this rise is a complex and of...

item.title: West Bengal repoll report alleges EVM tampering and voter tracking
item.description: The West Bengal repoll report has highlighted serious irregularities during the recent elections. According to the report, certain Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs) were allegedly taped to prevent vot...

item.title: Miami Grand Prix start time moved due to forecasted heavy rain
item.description: The Miami Grand Prix has had its start time moved forward by three hours due to the threat of severe weather, including heavy rain and thunderstorms. The decision, taken after consultations between Fo...

item.title: NU Nazareth secures final semifinal spot after beating FEU-D in UAAP U-19 3x3
item.description: NU Nazareth School clinched the last semifinal berth in the UAAP Season 88 Under-19 3x3 basketball tournament after defeating Far Eastern University-Diliman (FEU-D). The matchup served as a crucial kn...

item.title: WOODZ returns to the Philippines and performs 'Raining in Manila'
item.description: K-pop singer WOODZ, also known as Cho Seungyoun, made a special return to the Philippines recently, delighting fans with a live performance of his hit song 'Raining in Manila.' The event was part of h...

item.title: Kenya Met Forecast Shows Widespread Rainfall Patterns Across Regions in May
item.description: The Kenya Meteorological Department has released its May 2026 weather outlook, indicating that many parts of the country will experience near-average to above-average rainfall, especially during the f...

item.title: Suspended Public Servants Cost South Africa R800 Million Annually
item.description: A staggering R800 million of taxpayer funds is spent each year on the salaries of public servants who are on suspension, with many of them remaining suspended for years. This includes over 670 employe...

item.title: Downtown Toronto Scheduled Power Outage: Duration and Affected Areas
item.description: A planned power outage is set to affect portions of downtown Toronto, aimed at maintenance and infrastructure upgrades. Utility companies have provided residents and businesses with an estimated durat...

item.title: Nairobi Man Pays Tribute to Late Githurai Woman Through Poem
item.description: A young woman, Lorine Okore, tragically passed away in Githurai 44, Kiambu County, leaving family, friends, and the local community heartbroken. Her body was discovered in a trench, and while some rep...

item.title: A Romance and Business Partnership Built on Understanding Beyond Words
item.description: In 2013, Federica, a pastry chef from Milan, moved to a small town in Veneto to work with a Michelin-starred chef. It was there she met Oskar, an Australian chef, whose presence initially seemed tempo...

item.title: Sundowns aim for back-to-back wins against Polokwane
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns will face Polokwane City for the second time in just a few days, looking to build on their midweek 1-0 victory. The win keeps their title hopes alive as they remain at the top of the...

item.title: NYC Police Release Body-Cam Footage of Machete-Wielding Suspect's Fatal Shooting at Grand Central
item.description: New York City police have released body-camera footage of officers fatally shooting a man who had attacked three people with a machete at the 42nd Street-Grand Central subway station. The man, identif...
